ad_compliance_checkpoints.regulation: 景品表示法 (Act against Unjustifiable Premiums and Misleading Representations)
ad_compliance_checkpoints.description: Prohibits misleading representations about products and services. All advertising claims must be substantiated and not create false impressions of superior quality or value.
checks: No優良誤認表示 (misleading superior quality claims)
No有利誤認表示 (misleading advantageous claims)
Proper disclosure of conditions and limitations

ad_compliance_checkpoints.regulation: 薬機法 (Act on Securing Quality, Efficacy and Safety of Products)
ad_compliance_checkpoints.description: Regulates advertising of pharmaceuticals, medical devices, cosmetics, and quasi-drugs. Restricts health-related claims in non-medical product advertising.
checks: No unapproved efficacy or effect claims
No before/after imagery implying medical effects
Proper categorization of product claims (cosmetic vs. quasi-drug vs. pharmaceutical)

ad_compliance_checkpoints.regulation: 特定商取引法 (Specified Commercial Transactions Act)
ad_compliance_checkpoints.description: Governs mail-order sales, telemarketing, and online transactions. Requires specific disclosures in advertising materials.
checks: Price and payment terms clearly stated
Seller identity and contact information included

ad_compliance_checkpoints.description: Protects creative works used in advertising. All third-party content must be properly licensed or fall under fair use exceptions.

ad_compliance_checkpoints.regulation: 個人情報保護法 (Act on Protection of Personal Information)
ad_compliance_checkpoints.description: Governs the use of personal data in targeted advertising and customer communications.

brand_rules.title: Minimum clear space around logo
brand_rules.description: The primary logo must maintain a clear space equal to the height of the logomark on all sides. No other graphical elements, text, or edges may encroach on this space.
examples: Logo placed with adequate padding in email headers
Violation: logo cropped at the edge of a social media banner

brand_rules.title: Do not alter logo colors or proportions
brand_rules.description: The logo must not be stretched, rotated, recolored, or modified in any way. Only approved color variants (full color, monochrome white, monochrome black) are permitted.
examples: Using the monochrome white variant on dark backgrounds
Violation: applying a gradient overlay to the full-color logo

brand_rules.title: Use approved brand colors only
brand_rules.description: All marketing materials must use colors from the approved brand palette. Primary: #1A73E8 (Blue), #202124 (Dark Gray). Secondary: #34A853 (Green), #FBBC04 (Yellow), #EA4335 (Red). Neutral: #F8F9FA, #DADCE0, #5F6368.
examples: Landing page using primary blue for CTAs and dark gray for body text
Violation: using an off-brand purple as a primary accent color

brand_rules.title: Maintain sufficient contrast ratios
brand_rules.description: Text over colored backgrounds must meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements: at least 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text.
examples: White text (#FFFFFF) on primary blue (#1A73E8) passes at 4.6:1
Violation: light gray text on white background at 2.1:1 ratio

brand_rules.title: Use active, clear, and confident language
brand_rules.description: Marketing copy should be written in active voice, avoid jargon, and convey confidence without arrogance. Sentences should be concise and scannable.
examples: Violation: "It is possible that utilization of our solution may potentially yield results" (passive, vague)

brand_rules.title: Do not use superlatives without substantiation
brand_rules.description: Claims such as "best", "fastest", "#1", or "industry-leading" must be backed by verifiable data or third-party validation. Unsubstantiated superlatives violate both brand guidelines and advertising regulations.
examples: Violation: "The best platform on the market" (no supporting evidence)

brand_rules.title: Use approved typefaces
brand_rules.description: All marketing materials must use the approved typeface family. Primary: Inter for digital, Noto Sans JP for Japanese content. Fallback: system sans-serif. Display sizes follow the type scale defined in the brand guide.
examples: Website headings set in Inter Bold at 32px / 40px line height
Violation: using a decorative script font for body copy in a product brochure
